metadata.purpose: Primitive atomic propositions for git-agent-fsm verification
design_rule: State APs are evaluated from live git repository state at observation time.
Transition APs have names prefixed with '(transition)' and are set by matching the action_label of each step.
Use natural-language names that are unambiguous given the git workflow context.
Do not invent APs not grounded in observable git state or explicit action types.
Each AP name is used verbatim in property ASTs — changes here require updating GIT_PROPERTIES_AST.json.

current_state_aps.name: A backup reference or recovery tag exists for the pre-rewrite commit range.
current_state_aps.description: A git ref (backup tag, ORIG_HEAD, or reflog entry) still points at commits that were rewritten or removed.

current_state_aps.name: A branch reference moves backward to exclude commits that were previously reachable by policy.
current_state_aps.description: The branch tip has been moved to an ancestor, making previously reachable commits unreachable through the ref.

current_state_aps.name: A commit object that previously existed in reachable local history becomes irrecoverably unreachable.
current_state_aps.description: A commit is no longer reachable from any ref and is eligible for garbage collection with no backup.

current_state_aps.name: A detached-head repository mode.
current_state_aps.description: HEAD points to a commit SHA rather than a branch name (git status shows 'HEAD detached at ...').

current_state_aps.name: A maintainer-level role.
current_state_aps.description: The acting user has maintainer or admin privileges on the target repository.

current_state_aps.name: A network status indicating remote connectivity is available.
current_state_aps.description: The remote host is reachable and git network operations (fetch/push/pull) can proceed.

current_state_aps.name: A non-fast-forward update approval token is present for this action.
current_state_aps.description: An explicit --force, --force-with-lease, or equivalent flag/config that authorises a non-fast-forward update.

current_state_aps.name: A protected branch requires linear history.
current_state_aps.description: The branch protection rule mandates linear history (no merge commits), i.e. rebases are required over merges.

current_state_aps.name: A protected-branch classification.
current_state_aps.description: The target branch is classified as protected in the repository settings (e.g. main, release/*).

current_state_aps.name: A push attempt was rejected because local and remote histories diverged.
current_state_aps.description: The most recent push returned a rejection error indicating non-fast-forward or diverged histories.

current_state_aps.name: A queue-drain or operator-intervention state is required before execution resumes.
current_state_aps.description: The workflow is paused waiting for the operation queue to drain or for manual operator intervention.

current_state_aps.name: A remote write-protection or permission-denied status is active.
current_state_aps.description: The remote returned a permission-denied error; write access to the remote ref is blocked.

current_state_aps.name: A required immediate force push was missed.
current_state_aps.description: The workflow reached a state where an immediate force-push was required but a different action was taken first.

current_state_aps.name: A synchronization status where local and remote histories have diverged.
current_state_aps.description: Both local and remote have commits the other does not; a simple fast-forward in either direction is impossible.

current_state_aps.name: A synchronization status where local history is ahead of remote history.
current_state_aps.description: Local branch has commits not yet on the remote; a fast-forward push is possible.

current_state_aps.name: A synchronization status where no rebase is needed (already synchronized).
current_state_aps.description: Local and remote tracking branch point at the same commit; no rebase or merge is necessary.

current_state_aps.name: A workflow state in which an immediate force push is required.
current_state_aps.description: The workflow has entered the phase after a rebase where the next mandatory action is force-push.

current_state_aps.name: All commits selected for rebase are local-only and unpublished.
current_state_aps.description: Every commit in the rebase range exists only in the local repository and has never been pushed.

current_state_aps.name: An authorized/authenticated status allowing the guarded action.
current_state_aps.description: The current operation has passed authentication and authorisation checks for the guarded action.

current_state_aps.name: Any new work must be attached to a named branch before commit or push workflows continue.
current_state_aps.description: The repository is in detached HEAD mode; new commits must first be attached to a branch ref.

current_state_aps.name: Authentication credentials for remote write are valid.
current_state_aps.description: SSH key, HTTPS token, or other credential used for remote write operations is present and valid.

current_state_aps.name: Branch histories are diverged and require reconciliation.
current_state_aps.description: Local and upstream have conflicting histories; a rebase or merge reconciliation step is required.

current_state_aps.name: Changes targeting main are integrated only through reviewed merge or rebase workflows.
current_state_aps.description: No direct commits to main are allowed; all changes must arrive via a reviewed PR merge or rebase.

current_state_aps.name: Detached HEAD contains commits that are not attached to any named branch.
current_state_aps.description: HEAD is detached and the detached commits are not reachable from any branch ref.

current_state_aps.name: Local branch contains commits that are ahead of the remote tracking branch.
current_state_aps.description: git status reports 'ahead of origin/branch by N commits'; a push would add new commits to the remote.

current_state_aps.name: Local history is ahead of the remote branch and has unpublished commits.
current_state_aps.description: There are commits in the local branch that have not been pushed to the remote tracking branch.

current_state_aps.name: The action is executed under an approved protected-branch override policy.
current_state_aps.description: An explicit override policy (e.g. bypass allowlist) has been approved for the current action on a protected branch.

current_state_aps.name: The actor is authenticated and authorized for force-push on the target branch.
current_state_aps.description: The actor has the required credentials and explicit authorisation to perform a force-push on this specific branch.

current_state_aps.name: The branch is behind upstream or has local commits that must be replayed.
current_state_aps.description: The local branch either lags behind the remote or has commits that need to be replayed via rebase.

current_state_aps.name: The branch returns to a clean synchronized state with no unpublished commit debt.
current_state_aps.description: After publishing, the local branch and remote tracking branch are identical with no pending commits.

current_state_aps.name: The commit graph remains acyclic and does not introduce reference cycles.
current_state_aps.description: No commit is its own ancestor; the git DAG is free of cycles.

current_state_aps.name: The current checked-out branch is main.
current_state_aps.description: HEAD resolves to refs/heads/main (or master depending on repository default).

current_state_aps.name: The current depth of pending git workflow operations in the processing queue exceeds 64.
current_state_aps.description: The internal workflow queue has accumulated more than 64 pending operations.

current_state_aps.name: The number of retry attempts used by the workflow operation exceeds 3.
current_state_aps.description: The current operation has been retried more than 3 times without success.

current_state_aps.name: The repository repeatedly returns to a clean synchronized state over time.
current_state_aps.description: Over the course of the workflow, the repository reaches a clean sync state multiple times.

current_state_aps.name: The repository workflow state indicates that a force push is required before normal publishing can continue.
current_state_aps.description: The workflow flag for 'force-push required' is set; normal push is blocked until force-push completes.

current_state_aps.name: The target remote reference is reachable and satisfies push preconditions.
current_state_aps.description: The remote ref exists, is reachable, and all push preconditions (credentials, permissions, fast-forward) are met.

current_state_aps.name: The workflow repeatedly reaches a state with no unpublished local commit debt.
current_state_aps.description: The workflow repeatedly passes through states where the local branch has no unpublished commits.

current_state_aps.name: Those detached commits are eventually anchored to a named branch reference.
current_state_aps.description: Commits made while in detached HEAD mode are eventually reachable from a named branch.

current_state_aps.name: Updates to this branch must pass through a reviewed integration workflow.
current_state_aps.description: Branch protection requires all updates to arrive via a reviewed PR or integration pipeline.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A destructive history-rewrite action is just performed.
transition_aps.description: The action taken is a git operation that rewrites or removes existing commit history (e.g. reset --hard, rebase -i with drop, filter-branch).

transition_aps.name: (transition) A direct commit action on the current branch is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git commit' is executed directly on the currently checked-out branch.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A force-push action is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git push --force' or 'git push --force-with-lease' is executed (distinct from the bare label variant).

transition_aps.name: (transition) A merge action is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git merge' is executed to combine two branch histories.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A mutating git action is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A pending mutating operation (write/modify) is dequeued from the workflow operation queue.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git push' without force flags is executed (distinct from the bare label variant).

transition_aps.name: (transition) A public-history rewrite operation is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A rewrite of already-published (remote) commit history is executed, e.g. amending a pushed commit.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A rebase action that fails due to branch divergence is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git rebase' invocation exits with a conflict or rejection error caused by branch divergence.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A rebase action is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git rebase' command is initiated (may or may not complete successfully).

transition_aps.name: (transition) A rebase action that successfully resolves divergence is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A 'git rebase' completes without conflict and resolves the divergence between local and remote.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A rebase action that rewrites published commits is just performed.
transition_aps.description: The rebase replays commits that were previously pushed; this constitutes a public history rewrite.

transition_aps.name: (transition) A rebase operation that fails and leaves divergence unresolved is just performed.
transition_aps.description: A rebase exits in a failed state (conflict, abort) and the branch remains diverged.

transition_aps.name: (transition) An immediate force-push action to publish rewritten history is just performed.
transition_aps.description: After a rebase or reset, a force-push is executed immediately as the mandatory follow-up to publish the rewritten history.

transition_aps.name: (transition) Any push action to the remote repository is just performed.
transition_aps.description: Any push action (force or normal) to the remote is executed in this step.
